title: Apply: mutations
description: Apply your understanding of mutations in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these mutations would best be described as *beneficial*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a mutation that lowers a person’s risk of getting heart disease
a mutation that prevents a person’s blood from clotting normally
a mutation that does not affect a person’s observable traits

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these mutations would best be described as *beneficial*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a mutation that increases a mouse’s ability to blend in and hide from predators
a mutation that increases a dog’s chance of developing kidney stones
a mutation that has no effect on a rabbit’s observable traits

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these mutations would best be described as *beneficial*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a mutation that prevents a person from being infected by a virus that attacks the immune system
a mutation that increases a person’s risk of developing lung cancer
a mutation that has no effect on a person’s observable traits

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these mutations would best be described as *harmful*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a mutation that causes mucus to build up in a person’s lungs, making it hard for them to breathe
a mutation that lowers a person’s risk of developing diabetes
a mutation that has no effect on a person’s observable traits

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these mutations would best be described as *harmful*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a mutation that causes a cow’s bones to break easily
a mutation that increases the size of a fish’s spines, helping protect it from predators
a mutation that has no effect on a plant’s observable traits

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these mutations would best be described as *harmful*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a mutation that causes a horse to have an impaired immune system
a mutation that increases a lizard’s ability to blend in with its surroundings and hide from predators
a mutation that has no effect on a bird’s observable traits

assessment_items.question_content:
The figures below illustrate a mutation in an organism’s gene sequence. 

Figure 1 shows the normal nucleotide sequence of a gene. Figure 1 also shows the amino acids encoded by that gene. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Figure 2 shows the same gene, but with a mutation. Figure 2 shows the amino acids encoded by the mutant version of the gene.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which of these best predicts the effect of the mutation on the organism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Figure 1 shows the gene sequence A C T G T G C T A. This gene sequence is shown to encode a series of amino acids, which are represented by colored circles in the following order: yellow, green, blue.

Figure 2 shows the gene sequence A C T G T C C T A. This sequence is labeled gene sequence with mutation. The second C in the sequence is colored red, indicating that it is the site of the mutation. The gene sequence is shown to encode a series of amino acids, which are represented by colored circles in the following order: yellow, green, blue.

choices.content: The mutation will have a neutral effect.
The mutation will have a harmful effect.
The mutation will have a beneficial effect.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figures below illustrate a mutation in an organism’s gene sequence. 

Figure 1 shows the normal nucleotide sequence of a gene. Figure 1 also shows the amino acids encoded by that gene. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Figure 2 shows the same gene, but with a mutation. Figure 2 shows the amino acids encoded by the mutant version of the gene.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Which of these best predicts the effect of the mutation on the organism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Figure 1 shows the gene sequence A T C C G C T G T. This gene sequence is shown to encode a series of amino acids, which are represented by colored circles in the following order: pink, blue, yellow.

Figure 2 shows the gene sequence A T C C G C T G C. This sequence is labeled gene sequence with mutation. The last C in the sequence is colored red, indicating that it is the site of the mutation. The gene sequence is shown to encode a series of amino acids, which are represented by colored circles in the following order: pink, blue, yellow.

choices.content: The effect of the mutation will be neutral.
The effect of the mutation will be harmful.
The effect of the mutation will be beneficial.

assessment_items.question_content:
The figures below illustrate a mutation in an organism’s gene sequence. 

Figure 1 shows the normal nucleotide sequence of a gene. Figure 1 also shows the amino acids encoded by that gene. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Figure 2 shows the same gene, but with a mutation. Figure 2 shows the amino acids encoded by the mutant version of the gene.

[[☃ image 3]]

**Which term describes the likely effect of the mutation on the organism?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Figure 1 shows the gene sequence T A C G A G T C T. This gene sequence is shown to encode a series of amino acids, which are represented by colored circles in the following order: orange, blue, gray.

Figure 2 shows the gene sequence T A T G A G T C T. This sequence is labeled gene sequence with mutation. The second T in the sequence is colored red, indicating that it is the site of the mutation. The gene sequence is shown to encode a series of amino acids, which are represented by colored circles in the following order: orange, blue, gray.

choices.content: neutral
harmful
beneficial

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the following passage. Then, answer the question below.

>Humans have a gene called *CLCN1* which encodes the chloride channel protein. This protein forms tube-like structures in the cell membrane. Charged atoms move through these structures, helping with muscle function.

>A person has a mutation in the *CLCN1* gene. Because of this mutation, a large portion of the gene’s nucleotide sequence is missing.

**Which two statements describe the most likely results of the gene mutation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The person’s chloride channel protein will have a different structure, but this will not affect the protein’s function.
The person’s chloride channel protein will not function normally.
The person’s muscles will function normally.
The person’s muscles will function differently than those of a person without the mutation.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the following passage. Then, answer the question below.

>Plants have a gene called *LH* which encodes the GA 3-oxidase protein. This protein helps make a substance called gibberellin in a plant’s cells. Plants with more gibberellin tend to grow taller than plants with less gibberellin.

>A certain plant has a mutation in the *LH* gene. Because of this mutation, a large portion of the gene’s nucleotide sequence is missing.

**Which two statements describe the most likely results of the gene mutation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The plant’s GA 3-oxidase protein will not function normally.
The plant’s GA 3-oxidase protein will have a different structure, but this will not affect the protein’s function.
The plant’s height will be different as a result of the mutation.
The plant’s cells will make the normal amount of gibberellin.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the following passage. Then, answer the question below.

>Humans have a gene called *PAH* which encodes the PAH protein. This protein helps break down a substance called phenylalanine. Getting a certain amount of phenylalanine in the diet is healthy. However, if too much phenylalanine builds up in a person’s blood, it can have a negative effect on their health.

>A person has a mutation in the *PAH* gene. Because of this mutation, a large portion of the gene’s nucleotide sequence is missing.

**Which two statements describe the most likely results of the gene mutation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The person’s PAH protein will not function normally.
The person’s PAH protein will have a different structure, but this will not affect the protein’s function.
The person’s health will be negatively affected.
The person’s blood will contain a normal level of phenylalanine.
